---
title: "Code Generation and Repair Loop"
---

# Code Generation and Repair Loop

**Working seal:** `stack://code-generation-and-repair-loop/DBD637DB96`

**Enter:** Use when an AI system is helping write or modify software and generation should stay tied to execution evidence.


## Related Runes

- [1088 diff {Forge}](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1088) - A visible account of what changed between one state and another. Good diffs make review local; bad diffs turn truth into fog.
- [1146 benchmark](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1146) - A performance claim forced to meet measurement. Benchmarks are where speed rhetoric either cashes out or dies.
- [1172 invariant](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1172) - A law that must remain true while the rest of the system is allowed to move. If you cannot say the invariant, you probably do not yet understand the change.
- [1180 observability](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1180) - Enough internal evidence exists that you can infer what the system is doing from the outside. Not logs alone: legible state under pressure.
- [1485 reproducible](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1485) - A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.


## Related Spell Templates and References

- [Test Generation](../spells/test-generation.qmd)
- [Bug Diagnosis From Logs](../spells/bug-diagnosis-from-logs.qmd)
- [Safe Refactoring](../spells/safe-refactoring.qmd)
- [Stack Grammar](../reference/stack-grammar.qmd)
- [Failure Modes](../reference/failure-modes.qmd)
- [Canon Map](../reference/canon-map.qmd)
| Step | Spell    | Artifact                                                       | Advance when                                         |
| ---- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| 1    | Specify  | Precise task statement, constraints, interfaces, and non-goals | The target is testable                               |
| 2    | Draft    | Smallest plausible implementation or patch                     | The code compiles in principle and names assumptions |
| 3    | Run      | Build, test, lint, or type-check output                        | Actual failures are captured verbatim                |
| 4    | Diagnose | Likely cause and repair surface                                | The diagnosis uses only emitted evidence             |
| 5    | Repair   | Smallest diff addressing the diagnosed cause                   | The patch is explicit                                |
| 6    | Verify   | Check results and residual risk                                | Checks pass or residual risk is clearly bounded      |

## Loop or Recursion

```json
{
  "steps": [
    3,
    4,
    5,
    6
  ],
  "until": "Checks pass, error surface stabilizes without progress, or attempt budget is exhausted"
}
```


## On Fail

Escalate with spec, code diff, failing output, diagnostic notes, and unresolved assumptions.

## Why It Works

It separates invention from judgment and binds repair to execution evidence.
